assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12: '| Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met -- single license filter, multi-license filter, invalid license 400 response, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape all verified'. criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md each show '## Verdict: PASS' for their respective criteria.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 25-31 under 'Scope Containment -- PASS' list exactly: 'Modified: modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs (specified in Files to Modify)', 'Modified: modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs (specified in Files to Modify)', 'Created: tests/api/package.rs (specified in Files to Create)'. Summary table line 7 shows '| Scope Containment | PASS |'.

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5: '| Review Feedback | N/A | No reviews or comments exist on the PR'. No output files indicate any sub-task creation. The report only contains verification findings and no mention of created sub-tasks.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 output files (report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md) are read-only verification reports with analysis text. No output files indicate modification of any code files (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs or any other source files). The outputs are exclusively reports.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 19 states 'All checks pass' and presents the findings summary, but there is no mention of auto-merging, merge commands, or any action to merge the PR. The report closes with 'This comment was AI-generated by sdlc-workflow/verify-pr' (line 106), confirming it is a report for human review only.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 10: '| Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines'. Lines 45-47 under 'Sensitive Pattern Scan -- PASS': 'No sensitive patterns detected in added lines across 3 files. Scanned for hardcoded passwords, API keys/tokens, private keys, environment files, cloud provider credentials, and database credentials.'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | Only new test file added (`tests/api/package.rs`); no existing tests modified or deleted'. Lines 101-103 under 'Test Change Classification -- ADDITIVE': 'Only a new test file was added (tests/api/package.rs). No existing test files were modified or deleted. All test changes are purely additive.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Domain Findings section covers: Intent Alignment (lines 23-41: Scope Containment PASS, Diff Size PASS, Commit Traceability PASS); Security (lines 43-47: Sensitive Pattern Scan PASS); Correctness (lines 49-71: CI Status PASS, Acceptance Criteria PASS, Verification Commands N/A); Style/Conventions (lines 73-103: Convention Upgrade N/A, Repetitive Test Detection PASS, Test Documentation PASS, Eval Quality N/A, Test Change Classification ADDITIVE). All required sub-checks are present.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment (lines 25-41): file-by-file listing with exact paths and modification type. Security (lines 45-47): lists specific pattern categories scanned. Correctness (lines 57-68): per-criterion analysis with code-level detail (e.g., 'validate_license_param splits on commas', 'spdx::Expression::parse() rejects invalid identifiers mapped to AppError::BadRequest'). Style/Conventions (lines 79-103): names each of the 4 test functions with their doc comments. criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md provide additional deep code-level analysis per criterion.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 97-98: 'Eval Quality -- N/A' with 'No eval result reviews found on the PR.' Summary table line 13: '| Test Quality | PASS | All 4 test functions have doc comments; no repetitive tests detected ... Eval Quality: N/A'.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A per report.md line 97-98. No output files mention creation of eval failure sub-tasks. The only sub-task-related mention is in line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 5-6: '| Review Feedback | N/A | No reviews or comments exist on the PR' and '| Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'. These appear in the top-level summary table, not under any of the four domain sections (Intent Alignment, Security, Correctness, Style/Conventions), consistent with orchestrator-level determination.
